purpose: Decompose gold fair value and extract market-implied probability of fiscal dominance
components: US 10Y real yield (TIPS): -$40-60/oz per 10bp rise — strongest single factor
Central bank net purchases: +$8-12/oz per 100 tonnes annual (1,037-1,045t in 2023-24, structural shift)
ETF flows: +$3-5/oz per 100 tonnes net inflow
current_assessment: $400-600/oz residual unexplained by traditional factors implies ~60-70% market probability of fiscal dominance within 5 years. Central bank buying at 1,000+ tonnes/year is revealed-preference signal of reserve diversification.
limitations: Residual attribution contested between fiscal, geopolitical diversification, momentum, and model misspecification
Central bank buying may be one-time portfolio rebalancing (5% → 10% gold) not permanent preference shift
Model sensitivity coefficients are estimated with wide uncertainty bands
Post-Russia-sanctions reserve diversification is a compelling alternative to fiscal dominance thesis
